clinical_criteria: Two or more ICD-9 code 340 (Multiple Sclerosis) for Type 1 definitive case
MS-specific medications with associated dose, frequency, route or strength
Multiple sclerosis keyword in problem list for Type 2 possible case
Manual curation required for all possible/probable cases
Age 18 and over for study population
Text search in H&P, discharge summary, and all notes for cases
Absence of autoimmune disease codes and keywords for controls
algorithm_summary: The Multiple Sclerosis phenotype algorithm identifies definitive cases through two pathways: Type 1 requires two or more ICD-9 code 340 (MS), while Type 2 requires specific demyelinating disease codes (341.9, 323.9, 341.2) AND MS-specific medications (Avonex, Betaseron, Copaxone, Tysabri) with proper dosing AND 'multiple sclerosis' keywords. Possible cases require either demyelinating disease codes with MS keywords OR MS keywords in problem list only. Controls are defined by absence of numerous autoimmune disease codes and related keywords. The algorithm excludes patients with other autoimmune conditions to ensure specificity.
